sections.paragraph: pr.n. Cyrus, king of Persia, Ezr 1:1, Ezr 1:7, Ezr 1:8 Isa 44:28 45:1 2Ch 36:22, 23 2Ch 36:23 Dan 1:21 6:29 10:1 . The Greeks have remarked that the Persians called the sun by this name (see Ctesias ap. Plut. Artax. Opp. t. i. p. 1012, Etym. M. Κῦρος, κοῦρος, ἥλιος), and rightly so; for it is the Zend. khoro, hur, ahurô; Pers. خور, هور (compare Sanscr. sûrg, sûri, and the word most frequently used, sûrja); ־ֶשׁ is a termination added, as in דַּרְיָוֶשׁ.-As to the opinion lately brought forward by U. Möller (De Authentia Oraculorum Esaiæ, Havniæ, 1825, p. 209, sq.), that the name כֹּרֶשׁ in Isaiah does not signify the king Cyrus, but the people of Israel (i.q. כּשֶׁר uprightness), it is needless to do more than mention it.
